supernovasky 2025-05-22 6:08 p.m.
Just a quick and heads-up that we are working more on building out Meshcore down here in the Puget Sound area now too because Meshtastic is a mess. We don't have things linked up around the region yet but hopefully will have some good progress within the next two or three months.

11:12 a.m.

Brock 2025-07-27 12:18 p.m.
I'm in Kamloops, so even bw 62.5 isn't going to cut it... We're messing around with paths to Vernon, Salmon Arm and Kelowna. Mt Lolo seems to have a good path that way. A casual test while working on VHF repeater gear confirmed it can be done, but a rain cell moved through and completely obliterated the path. 910 appears to be sensitive to that. 433 would cut through much better. As hams we can use it. Might try bridging 910 and 433 Heltec devices.
1
12:20 p.m.
I'll be interested to see how your water crossings manage heavy precipitation.
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-07-27 12:25 p.m.
Very much so
12:25 p.m.
And winter on little solar panels lol
ded (SalishMesh)
And winter on little solar panels lol
Brock 2025-07-27 12:35 p.m.
Luckily we have grid power up there! But repeaters for other sites are being prepared with solar panels. Vertical orientation to shed snow and maximize lower sun angle during winter.
1
ded (SalishMesh)
They seem high because we are testing BW62.5 and SF7
Brock 2025-07-27 2:46 p.m.
Can you tell me more about how you settled on this BW and SF? Did you try other combinations first? How did you notify/convince everyone to switch over (assuming they have)?
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-07-27 2:48 p.m.
Last q first, in lucky and no one else is using it here yet. There is one repeater down town that has a bad one way link. Even if I stand beside the building it's supposed to be on, my SNR to it is terrible so something is up.
2:49 p.m.
I was using the North America default at first and saw Puget testing the narrow BW so tested as well. In my local area with home repeater, after changing I could reach previous deaspots and get messages through in a larger area so overall general improvement. Speed was much better as well.
2:50 p.m.
I trust cisen as he knows a lot about this kind of thing and does proper test etc. for example he found that the narrow bw squeezed between the power meter noise we all share, you could see it on his sdr
2:51 p.m.
So basically re last q, I'm setting the stage and others can join if they choose
Brock 2025-07-27 3:00 p.m.
@ded (SalishMesh) thanks for that. Our group up here are mostly radio guys, so we're more interested in coverage and reliability than the underlying firmware. And not even that interested in chatting with it either, as far as that goes! The biggest challenge I have with it is understanding what the spectrum looks like without an S meter or waterfall display. SNR is great, but it's rarely consistent, so inferring what's happening is not simple. I will take my TinySA Ultra up the mountain next time and see what it can tell me. Given the standard settings are what the new users will use, getting the word out about non-default settings is the challenge. I've also wondered about adjusting the frequency a bit too... But need to investigate thoroughly with the SA first.
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-07-27 3:08 p.m.
You should coordinate with cisien, he is also wondering if the frequency is the best choice (edited)
3:08 p.m.
I think he should also have some waterfall shots to share as I've seen them before
Brock 2025-07-27 3:11 p.m.
I can certainly do that. ISM is a pretty messy part of the spectrum. I don't think it's about finding a quiet spot, but more likely, the least terrible spot!
1

thisagaingm 2025-07-28 4:32 p.m.
Hi, all. Anyone else in the general Olympia area? I’m putting together a small network here, with a plan to use Reticulum for a larger one. I’d rather add to your existing infrastructure where possible. I have a few repeaters built with 2, 3, and 5 dbi antennae, and have a 5.8 I have not tried. I’d be willing to put that one at a the right altitude with a little guidance. (edited)
thisagaingm
Hi, all. Anyone else in the general Olympia area? I’m putting together a small network here, with a plan to use Reticulum for a larger one. I’d rather add to your existing infrastructure where possible. I have a few repeaters built with 2, 3, and 5 dbi antennae, and have a 5.8 I have not tried. I’d be willing to put that one at a the right altitude with a little guidance. (edited)
mcarper 2025-07-28 4:44 p.m.
The Puget sound area is on Frequency 910.525 MHz Bandwidth 62.5 kHz Spreading Factor 7 Coding Rate 5 This is the big repeater on Cougar. If you can get to this you should get to Canada and on very lucky days portland
1
Dan - WI7FI [PNW] 2025-07-28 4:44 p.m.
i wonder if anywhere in or around olympia can see cougar
1
thisagaingm 2025-07-28 4:45 p.m.
I am running these settings now on my equipment. From my house, and my repeaters around town, I do not see it. I’ll try to get one up in the Black Hills and see what we can see.
4:45 p.m.
I also have a tbeam coming in today, and maybe those up north might be able to see me. Thank you all.
mcarper 2025-07-28 4:46 p.m.
https://store.rokland.com/products/12-dbi-aya-9012-alfa-network-n-female-directional-yagi-outdoor-iot-915-mhz-antenna might help if you can see the hills east of downtown seattle
ALFA Network 12 dBi N-Female Directional Yagi Outdoor IoT 915 MHz Ante
ALFA Network introduces the 12 dBi Directional Yagi Antenna tuned for the 915 MHz frequency for IoT. This antenna has a narrow, directional beamwidth which will perform well in IoT operated situations needing long-range in a specific direction with a significant line of sight. This antenna does meet the US 915 MHz requ

mcarper 2025-07-28 4:51 p.m.
If you can get on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Capitol_Peak_(Thurston_County) in the black hills that'd be amazing
Capitol Peak (Thurston County)
Capitol Peak is a 2,658-foot (810 m) peak in the Black Hills of the Capitol State Forest in the U.S. state of Washington. It is one of the two highest peaks in the Black Hills, along with Larch Mountain and the third highest point in Thurston County. There is a partially paved road to the summit called the Sherman Valley Road. The summit hosts a...

jaberwocky(he/him)-VancouverBC 2025-07-28 4:59 p.m.
We need to get Vancouver to decide on/agree with the settings though. All of the Vancouver nodes on the internet map are 250kHz SF11 currently (I'm using that because someone told me to stick to the defaults to start) but you're advocating for 62.5kHz, SF7.
Dan - WI7FI [PNW] 2025-07-28 5:01 p.m.
in the experience of folks in Seattle, the narrowband preset which is now a selectable preset in the app (62.5kHz/SF7) works much better
5:01 p.m.
and has 2.5x more throughput
5:01 p.m.
than the 250kHz/sf11 preset
Dan - WI7FI [PNW]
in the experience of folks in Seattle, the narrowband preset which is now a selectable preset in the app (62.5kHz/SF7) works much better
Dan - WI7FI [PNW] 2025-07-28 5:02 p.m.
it theoretically has less link budget than the wider preset, but it seems to cut through interference that's all over the 900MHz band much better (edited)
jaberwocky(he/him)-VancouverBC
We need to get Vancouver to decide on/agree with the settings though. All of the Vancouver nodes on the internet map are 250kHz SF11 currently (I'm using that because someone told me to stick to the defaults to start) but you're advocating for 62.5kHz, SF7.
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-07-28 5:03 p.m.
They are just behind on the latest experiment
Dan - WI7FI [PNW] 2025-07-28 5:03 p.m.
so in practice, nodes that were once marginal into cougar on wide are now for the most part, solid into cougar on narrow

jaberwocky(he/him)-VancouverBC 2025-07-28 7:15 p.m.

jaberwocky(he/him)-VancouverBC 2025-07-28 7:24 p.m.

Brock 2025-07-28 7:25 p.m.
Yes, so unless there are nodes in that direction who are using the experimental settings, you're more likely to have some success with the defaults.

8:54 p.m.

ded (SalishMesh)

Brock 2025-08-06 12:21 p.m.
It will be interesting to see what more elevation and gain might do over the longer hops, but I suspect that the limitation will be the frequency and realistic power output. 433 is where the long jumps need to be. I believe the firmware supports bridging on some devices - the Heltec V3 being one. Unfortunately 433 is Ham. But there are devices that operate there (like garage door openers) that seem to be ignored by ISED. I'm not suggesting that regulations be broken, but it sure would be interesting to compare performance...
Cisien 2025-08-06 12:22 p.m.
I have 108km from cougar to erie coming in pretty reliably
12:22 p.m.
so you can expect at least that with a clear fresnel zone
1
Cisien
I have 108km from cougar to erie coming in pretty reliably
Brock 2025-08-06 12:24 p.m.
But have you run it during a winter rain or snowstorm? The Fresnel zone gets... complicated...in those conditions.

i bet
12:24 p.m.
we'll find out in a few months

Brock 2025-08-06 12:29 p.m.
Indeed! I did some informal testing at 6000 ft after doing some VHF repeater stuff. The solid shot I had completely disappeared when a storm cell crossed in between. Just one cloud - clear and sunny either side. I was surprised at just how impactful it was.

Deleted User 2025-08-09 3:14 p.m.
Mount Tuam or Bruce on Salt Spring would provide decent coverage on both sides. Two-way radio outfits use Bruce for providing wide area coverage between say Nanaimo, Victoria and everything in between along the eastern side of the island. Heck I can get into VHF/UHF repeaters on Bruce from just about anywhere in the lower mainland.
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-08-09 3:39 p.m.

ded (SalishMesh) 2025-08-09 9:29 p.m.

Cisien 2025-08-09 9:29 p.m.
-120 is the minimum the metric goes to

9:31 p.m.

9:31 p.m.

9:32 p.m.

11:11 p.m.

ded (SalishMesh) 2025-08-09 11:21 p.m.

Cisien 2025-08-09 11:21 p.m.
910.250 has the lowest noise floor for me at home
11:26 p.m.

ded (SalishMesh) 2025-08-09 11:26 p.m.

11:27 p.m.

11:27 p.m.

11:31 p.m.

RadioManCN89

jaberwocky(he/him)-VancouverBC 2025-08-10 7:12 p.m.
I should be able to sort of see UBC Exchange through the trees once you have it up.
Cisien 2025-08-10 7:48 p.m.
maybe that's a lot of terrain in the way
Dan - WI7FI [PNW] 2025-08-10 8:53 p.m.
if more than 30% of the fresnel zone is blocked it's likely to be flaky or not work at all (edited)
8:53 p.m.
I think the inner lines are 10% and 30%
8:54 p.m.
keep in mind that these elevation maps rarely account for buildings as well
Cisien 2025-08-10 8:54 p.m.
i think this site uses some baselines for areas that are within city limits and labeled as forrests
Dan - WI7FI [PNW]
if more than 30% of the fresnel zone is blocked it's likely to be flaky or not work at all (edited)
Dan - WI7FI [PNW] 2025-08-10 8:55 p.m.
this is also a big reason why getting your antenna up off the ground a dozen or so feet, and in free space as much as possible will make a gigantic difference in who can hear you and what you can hear
1
jaberwocky(he/him)-VancouverBC

2:42 p.m.
that GPS modul says its low power
2:42 p.m.
like 32micro amps
2:47 p.m.

Cisien 2025-08-15 3:01 p.m.
yeah, not entirely sure how active they are, ultra low is probably sleep mode
RadioManCN89 2025-08-15 3:44 p.m.
there is a pin on the chip to put it into low power mode "Internal pull-up, pull down to shut down the module, keep high level or hang for normal operation"

MrAlders0n 2025-08-29 12:03 p.m.
Hey Guys, I hopped in here a few weeks ago (with my old handle) talking about putting a repeater up in the mountains in Squamish(when I visit my buddy in Oct). I need some guidance from anyone who has ran a mountain node on build requirements. Here in Ottawa I use a rak unify box and a 3000mah lithium ion battery but I’m feeling like I should use LTO and a larger solar panel given the lack of sun in the winter. Any tips would be great (edited)
12:04 p.m.
Any recommendations for anyone who has deployed a repeater in the mountains would be appreciated
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-08-29 1:42 p.m.

1:42 p.m.

mcarper 2025-08-29 2:12 p.m.

mcarper 2025-09-04 12:31 p.m.
Brock 2025-09-04 5:46 p.m.
Anyone who might be interested...we activated a new repeater on Mt Lolo on Monday. That was the missing link to connect through to Vernon and further south into the Okanagan. It doesn't seem like the links are super reliable yet though. And yes, we are running the 62.5 BW etc. It really seems like the repeaters stop adverting after a while. Either that or the app isn't decoding the traffic properly. For example, the Mt Lolo repeater hasn't been heard adverting since the first...but I'm hearing other repeater adverts that would be impossible if Lolo wasn't repeating. I'm not sure what to think about this.
1
Brock
Anyone who might be interested...we activated a new repeater on Mt Lolo on Monday. That was the missing link to connect through to Vernon and further south into the Okanagan. It doesn't seem like the links are super reliable yet though. And yes, we are running the 62.5 BW etc. It really seems like the repeaters stop adverting after a while. Either that or the app isn't decoding the traffic properly. For example, the Mt Lolo repeater hasn't been heard adverting since the first...but I'm hearing other repeater adverts that would be impossible if Lolo wasn't repeating. I'm not sure what to think about this.
🌲 Tree 2025-09-04 5:52 p.m.
If the signal isn't great, adverts will fail much more often. Adverts are very large packets (requiring the radio to tx longer) and fail with higher frequency due to collisions/interference/etc. Another possibility if it was restarted for any reason is the clock could be wrong. Adverts with a clock too far incorrect are disregarded
Brock 2025-09-04 5:56 p.m.
Yup, I'm aware of the time sync requirement. I suppose it's possible that there was a power blip on the mountain that rebooted the repeater, but based on other equipment on the same power source, I don't think that happened. Lolo is very strong into town, so the fact that adverts it repeats from a hop or two beyond are heard regularly, but Lolo is not, seems suspect.
🌲 Tree 2025-09-04 5:56 p.m.
Yeah that is a strange one
Brock 2025-09-04 5:57 p.m.
I've also had my personal repeater that sits about 10 ft away from me also go quiet on adverts... It doesn't make sense. (edited)
5:57 p.m.
It almost seems like logging in and manually adverting will get it going again automatically... But I'm not fully convinced of that.
5:58 p.m.
It might be coincidence...
🌲 Tree 2025-09-04 6:02 p.m.
If you're able to remote admin, might be worth syncing the clock (it'll say it doesn't need syncing if it's fine) and double checking the advert intervals config?
Brock 2025-09-04 6:05 p.m.
I'll have to see if I can get the credentials for that one. The builder didn't share yet. But if it's like mine, the intervals are always there and correct and the time is correct. It just decides to stop adverting without any apparent cause. Either that or the app gets flaky and stops reporting the latest updates heard.
🌲 Tree 2025-09-04 6:06 p.m.
I do think that your companion needs to be connected to update last heard
Brock 2025-09-04 6:07 p.m.
Are you sure about that? I'm pretty sure I've seen updates when I reconnect the Bluetooth connection if the companion has been powered up while I'm away.
🌲 Tree 2025-09-04 6:07 p.m.
I think that it will add new contacts, if you have that turned on, but it doesn't keep the adverts
6:07 p.m.
If it's already added
Brock 2025-09-04 6:07 p.m.
Interesting...
Uncle Lit 2025-09-04 6:07 p.m.
if the guest password is not set, you maybe able to log in to that repeater as guest
6:08 p.m.
very happy to see more folks in BC are on narrow/recommended!
2
Brock 2025-09-04 6:08 p.m.
I have noticed that I can see an up-to-date advert on the list, but if I go into the details of that node it will show last heard as being may of last year. I know that's probably the default date but why is it showing an up-to-date one on the list but not there? (edited)
🌲 Tree 2025-09-04 6:08 p.m.
This is a reason I like my tdeck as an independent witness. Because it doesn't care about any other devices to log everything properly
Brock
I have noticed that I can see an up-to-date advert on the list, but if I go into the details of that node it will show last heard as being may of last year. I know that's probably the default date but why is it showing an up-to-date one on the list but not there? (edited)
🌲 Tree 2025-09-04 6:09 p.m.
I'm not 100% sure how that field works but my last heard field is often a lie as well. Maybe @Liam Cottle would know what's going on there
6:09 p.m.
I can many times go to discover list and see recent adverts and last heard on the contact details screen would not be updated correctly (edited)
Brock 2025-09-04 6:11 p.m.
It sure seems like there's some contradictions between different views of last heard information.
1
6:11 p.m.
Similar with the mapping page... Nodes can move around and even go back to where they were previously for no apparent reason. I haven't been able to get my head around what's going on with that only that it's often wrong with no apparent reason.
🌲 Tree 2025-09-04 6:13 p.m.
Though sometimes last heard does update when a fresh advert comes in. Maybe that depends on the phone being connected? Idk. Definitely something strange happening in some situations causing this
6:14 p.m.
I've noticed it enough times that now when I cleanup old contacts, I check discover list as the source of truth for last heard because I don't fully trust the contact detail field
Brock 2025-09-04 6:29 p.m.
I'm glad it's not just me...
mcarper 2025-09-04 7:27 p.m.

mcarper 2025-09-07 11:10 p.m.
2x4s and 4-5" screws and a 3m rope around the tree; with that you can build a ladder up the tree as high as you want and then put the node up there I would just start the ladder 4 m off the ground so bring a small ladder with you before building the wood ladder on the tree. https://www.harborfreight.com/14-ft-reach-type-i-250-lb-portable-telescoping-ladder-56729.html (edited)
14 ft. Reach, Type I, 250 lb. Portable Telescoping Ladder
ka.RFz 2025-09-07 11:12 p.m.
Mt Work is a park too so need to be discreet with any structures
1
mcarper 2025-09-07 11:15 p.m.
6" long 2x4 lumber 2 screws pre-set in a bag. Build as you climb up

ALTER TABLE meshcore_packets DELETE WHERE bitAnd(bitShiftRight(reinterpretAsUInt8(substring(packet, 1, 1)), 2), 0x0F) = 4 AND hex(substring( packet, 3 + reinterpretAsUInt8(substring(packet, 2, 1)), 32 )) = '42FE31AFFC54F7FDCFA0129F5C78B753FB9917F4AB1AFBC9E42037789E57CA9A'; lmao

Brock 2025-09-10 4:28 p.m.

ka.RFz 2025-09-11 6:57 p.m.
We are in talks to get a repeater installed at the Mt Gardner radio site on Bowen Island. That will provide a nice LOS path to Nanaimo and adjacent communities up and down the coast. As well, Gardner has LOS into Squamish. The guy who manages the site was actually helicoptering onto the mountain yesterday and asked if we had one ready to go. Doh, a little short notice to make it work but next time for sure.
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-09-11 6:58 p.m.
Nice and if we get hull or Brenton
jaberwocky(he/him)-VancouverBC 2025-09-12 1:01 a.m.

ka.RFz 2025-09-12 11:49 a.m.

ka.RFz 2025-09-15 9:50 p.m.
@Cisien @ded (SalishMesh) I got mctomqtt running. Though it's blasting the mqtt server with the following pretty aggressively. I compiled the firmware with packet logging + debug. I assume this is probably some room server related code looping.. 2025-09-15 18:41:29,728 - DEBUG - RX: DEBUG: loop - checking for client 0A 2025-09-15 18:41:29,733 - DEBUG - From Radio: DEBUG: loop - checking for client 0A 2025-09-15 18:41:29,745 - DEBUG - Published to meshcore/debug: {"origin": "UBC EM RS A\r", "origin_id": "A1D392F9459CB8F4E17B0A8F2802CA08EDD6F1BF626D4E4E2015E89DEDD5B89D\r", "timestamp": "2025-09-15T18:41:29.739014", "type": "DEBUG", "message": "DEBUG: loop - checking for client 0A"} (edited)
Cisien 2025-09-15 9:50 p.m.
don't enable debug
9:51 p.m.
you only need MESH_PACKET_LOGGING
ka.RFz 2025-09-15 9:52 p.m.
ok cool. I guess I misinterpreted what @ded (SalishMesh) said about having debug enabled. Will recompile and try again
Cisien 2025-09-15 9:53 p.m.
i think one of the projects are playing with the debug spam, but i don't think it's important for much of anything. Cougar doesn't use it
1
ka.RFz 2025-09-15 9:54 p.m.
copy
9:59 p.m.
Ok Looks like we are in business here. Thanks
10:00 p.m.

jaberwocky(he/him)-VancouverBC 2025-09-20 1:25 p.m.
It's it's own city though. It's kind of like the Redmond of Vancouver. North Vancouver is the Renton of Vancouver. Burnaby, Surrey, and Richmond are all three like three Bellevues of Vancouver.
1
1
ka.RFz 2025-09-20 1:31 p.m.
Yeah the City of West Vancouver often confuses visitors. It's not attached to the City of Vancouver but rather separated by water and just to confuse things, UBC is on the west side of the City of Vancouver but not actually a part of the city. lol
jaberwocky(he/him)-VancouverBC 2025-09-20 1:45 p.m.
The map is complicated. Railroad tycoons and corruption over a 100 years ago set up many of the city centers and names and we're still living with many of those choices.
jaberwocky(he/him)-VancouverBC
The map is complicated. Railroad tycoons and corruption over a 100 years ago set up many of the city centers and names and we're still living with many of those choices.

jaberwocky(he/him)-VancouverBC 2025-09-20 2:05 p.m.
https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/William_Cornelius_Van_Horne
William Cornelius Van Horne
Sir William Cornelius Van Horne, (February 3, 1843 – September 11, 1915) was an American businessman, industrialist and railroad magnate who spent most of his career in Canada. He is famous for overseeing the construction of the first Canadian transcontinental railway, a project that was completed in 1885, in under half the projected time. He...
ka.RFz 2025-09-20 2:56 p.m.
I'd like to experiment with a few different antennas on these repeaters. VE7UBC North, VE7NWR New West and the KA RAK4631 test repeater all use the same or similar 8dBi omni. From looking at the packet analyzer over the past few days, the receive sensitivity doesn't seem nearly as good as it should be when comparing to other repeaters down south. The vertical beamwidth on the 8dBi units are pretty narrow so that is may be playing into it. Would be good to get a run down of what antennas you all are using at your high/medium elevation sites.
ka.RFz
I'd like to experiment with a few different antennas on these repeaters. VE7UBC North, VE7NWR New West and the KA RAK4631 test repeater all use the same or similar 8dBi omni. From looking at the packet analyzer over the past few days, the receive sensitivity doesn't seem nearly as good as it should be when comparing to other repeaters down south. The vertical beamwidth on the 8dBi units are pretty narrow so that is may be playing into it. Would be good to get a run down of what antennas you all are using at your high/medium elevation sites.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-09-20 3:18 p.m.

1

ka.RFz 2025-09-20 8:15 p.m.

Cisien 2025-09-20 8:15 p.m.

ka.RFz 2025-09-20 8:17 p.m.
Yeah probably a Sinclair.
8:17 p.m.
I like the idea of the null fill models they have.
8:17 p.m.
https://www.sinctech.com/collections/base-station-antennas?pf_t_antenna_band_category=869+-+960+MHz&pf_t_antenna_type=Collinear
Cisien 2025-09-20 8:17 p.m.
it's a typical omni, possibly collinear
ka.RFz 2025-09-20 8:19 p.m.
Yeah the paging / SCADA outfits use these around here as well.
8:20 p.m.
Most of our VHF/UHF repeaters are also using Sinclair or Comprod stuff
8:24 p.m.
Would also be interesting to try a panel / sectoral antenna up at one of our mountain top sites. It would make sense to use one on say Mt Seymour here as the tower is technically on the side of the mountain, not the top so there is no point in radiating ~160deg into the side of the mountain with a typical omni.
8:29 p.m.
8:31 p.m.
Looks like the null fill models have orderable electrical downtilt options. Perfect for mountain top use where a typical omni overshoots your desired coverage area.
Cisien 2025-09-20 8:32 p.m.
yeah, maybe

Cisien 2025-09-24 1:32 a.m.
What would a minimal advert be? Name and location and key, without signature?
1
1:34 a.m.
Looking at roughly 1/2 the size if you ditch the signature (assuming path is only a few bytes/hops) (edited)
1:35 a.m.
But that opens it up to impersonation if not careful
mcarper 2025-09-24 1:54 a.m.
Would work for repeaters
Cisien 2025-09-24 2:00 a.m.
You would still want some way to validate a repeater identity
🌲 Tree 2025-09-24 2:26 a.m.
Well adverts are what like 120-130 bytes or so. Big but not so big they should be a problem. They can be even smaller if you keep the name short and location off. That size is easily reached by text messages too - unless you stick to just a few emojis, and they'd have the same reliability
2:28 a.m.
Public key and signature I think are bytes you can't really afford to lose without compromising on security (from multiple angles)
ded (SalishMesh)
Click to see attachment 🖼️
Dan - WI7FI [PNW] 2025-09-24 3:27 a.m.
banana for scale?
🌲 Tree
Public key and signature I think are bytes you can't really afford to lose without compromising on security (from multiple angles)

🌲 Tree
Well adverts are what like 120-130 bytes or so. Big but not so big they should be a problem. They can be even smaller if you keep the name short and location off. That size is easily reached by text messages too - unless you stick to just a few emojis, and they'd have the same reliability

🌲 Tree 2025-09-24 4:32 p.m.
Just remember many emojis are multiple times the size of normal chars. You can measure here: https://mothereff.in/byte-counter but "" is 4 bytes - just the emoji, no spaces
UTF-8 string length & byte counter
An on-the-fly UTF-8 byte counter.
4:33 p.m.
"‍‍‍" is 25 bytes lol
4:34 p.m.

4:35 p.m.
"" = 35 bytes. The largest emoji
Panic 2025-09-24 5:07 p.m.
"" (4 bytes) is half the size of "Repeater" (8 bytes), so it's an improvement ;)

Dan - WI7FI [PNW] 2025-09-24 6:05 p.m.

Brock 2025-09-24 11:37 p.m.
We've got a pretty solid link between Kamloops and Vernon now. And beyond, but nobody in Kelowna or Salmon Arm seems to be active yet. Four repeaters involved. Adding all the distances between them would sum to over 100 km, between mountaintops. Some behavior I've noticed and wonder if the Salish hops see similar...: I often see 'delivered' appear very shortly after the second attempt begins. I'm wondering if the second attempt is made before the ack from the first one can get back to me? Similarly, red failures sometimes flip to green delivered. Is my guess reasonable? Does the code look at the route and determine a realistic delay to use before a retry is sent?
11:39 p.m.
Or is it a fixed value after the transmission is completed the first time?
Brock
We've got a pretty solid link between Kamloops and Vernon now. And beyond, but nobody in Kelowna or Salmon Arm seems to be active yet. Four repeaters involved. Adding all the distances between them would sum to over 100 km, between mountaintops. Some behavior I've noticed and wonder if the Salish hops see similar...: I often see 'delivered' appear very shortly after the second attempt begins. I'm wondering if the second attempt is made before the ack from the first one can get back to me? Similarly, red failures sometimes flip to green delivered. Is my guess reasonable? Does the code look at the route and determine a realistic delay to use before a retry is sent?
ka.RFz 2025-09-25 12:14 a.m.
What all did you guys end up tweaking to improve things? Also did you guys end up giving up on Lolo and pick a different site?
Brock
We've got a pretty solid link between Kamloops and Vernon now. And beyond, but nobody in Kelowna or Salmon Arm seems to be active yet. Four repeaters involved. Adding all the distances between them would sum to over 100 km, between mountaintops. Some behavior I've noticed and wonder if the Salish hops see similar...: I often see 'delivered' appear very shortly after the second attempt begins. I'm wondering if the second attempt is made before the ack from the first one can get back to me? Similarly, red failures sometimes flip to green delivered. Is my guess reasonable? Does the code look at the route and determine a realistic delay to use before a retry is sent?
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-09-25 12:22 a.m.
I've seen the same behavior. The choice for try to estimate the time but sometimes it can be longer and still arrive
ka.RFz
What all did you guys end up tweaking to improve things? Also did you guys end up giving up on Lolo and pick a different site?
Brock 2025-09-25 1:03 a.m.
Lolo is a good site. Quiet. Good line of sight to several other sites. Dufferin was a lost cause. We've set different TX delays on each repeater. And went with a really short AGC reset value, as most of them have MT built into the same unit too. I suspect both tweaks were important.
ded (SalishMesh)
I've seen the same behavior. The choice for try to estimate the time but sometimes it can be longer and still arrive
Brock 2025-09-25 1:05 a.m.
It's a tough one to figure out I'd guess! It also seems like the timeout between attempts doesn't factor in incoming messages monopolizing the radio.
mcarper 2025-09-25 1:05 a.m.
AGC 4 is needed when next to MT
mcarper
AGC 4 is needed when next to MT
Brock 2025-09-25 1:06 a.m.
I don't remember the number, but that sounds about right. My preference would be to abandon the MT completely, but they aren't my devices.
Brock
Or is it a fixed value after the transmission is completed the first time?
Cisien 2025-09-25 1:09 a.m.
The app estimates based on the lora settings, path length and some padding. Long txdelay along the path isnt lnown
Cisien
The app estimates based on the lora settings, path length and some padding. Long txdelay along the path isnt lnown
Brock 2025-09-25 1:11 a.m.
It would be great if there was a way to include that. I get the feeling that acks might be causing collisions...?
Cisien 2025-09-25 1:11 a.m.
Try running nightly on your companions
1:12 a.m.
There was some fixes around paths (edited)
1:12 a.m.
Responses going out as flood instead of direct
Cisien
Responses going out as flood instead of direct
Brock 2025-09-25 10:26 a.m.
That could certainly be related - thanks!
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-09-26 9:09 p.m.
Cool, they realized they are not alone

ka.RFz 2025-09-27 5:32 p.m.
There are also quite a few new users to the mesh up here who may have poor coverage to the closest repeater, who don't realize their messages are propagating southbound and have no idea about the hashtag channels. Will try to get the word out though. Other than a few very random messages on public when the propagation is good, we don't see much from Seattle. Seems very one way usually when looking at the packet analyzer.

ka.RFz 2025-10-01 1:04 a.m.
@Deleted User Can't reply to your msg on public, I sadly don't have coverage at my place yet, need to figure out how/where to install a repeater down by my place. Can get in via the dyke though. There is no best/verified solution out there. Everyone builds / buys kits that fit their needs really. Some go with the Station G2 due to its higher transmit power output but its not a great solution if you need to solar power it.
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-10-01 10:16 a.m.
And they should only be used where needed as the higher power can create one way links
1
Deleted User 2025-10-01 11:44 a.m.
Good to know! Thanks @ka.RFz and @ded (SalishMesh) I have PoE at the roof of my building, So power is not a big concern at this location. I’m currently running a simple heltec v3 with an Alfa 915 antenna. Perhaps a higher mount would yield better results than throwing money at better equipment.
Deleted User
Good to know! Thanks @ka.RFz and @ded (SalishMesh) I have PoE at the roof of my building, So power is not a big concern at this location. I’m currently running a simple heltec v3 with an Alfa 915 antenna. Perhaps a higher mount would yield better results than throwing money at better equipment.
ka.RFz 2025-10-01 11:46 a.m.

Deleted User 2025-10-01 11:54 a.m.

RadioManCN89 2025-10-01 12:01 p.m.
there is a lot of land between seafair and yaletown
Deleted User 2025-10-01 12:08 p.m.
I was looking at the line of sight map, I don’t know how high the yaletown antenna is, but I figure above 130 meters in a tall building, it barely goes over the line of sight obstacles to my repeater.
12:08 p.m.
Still cool that I can hear and reply back, even if not super reliably
12:09 p.m.
It’s like 15 km away.
RadioManCN89 2025-10-01 12:31 p.m.

12:32 p.m.
12:33 p.m.

12:34 p.m.
you would be supprized the distance you can get with airplane scatter on VHF freqs
Deleted User 2025-10-01 12:57 p.m.

A python based script to send meshore debug and packet capture data to MQTT for analysis Requires meshcore repeater to be connected to a raspberry pi, server or similar linux device able to run py...

ded (SalishMesh) 2025-10-01 3:52 p.m.

Brock 2025-10-01 4:55 p.m.
The thing about gain is you can't magically increase the effective power without giving something up. A Yagi's dB rating is based on concentrating the radiated power in one direction, so it has a dB rating above that of the theoretical isotropic radiator. In the case of an omnidirectional antenna with a higher gain, that typically means that the signal is more concentrated horizontally, which means receivers higher and lower than the plane of signal will suffer. So if you want to pursue gain numbers, you need to recognize your compromise. There's no magic and there's no free lunch, unfortunately.
Cisien 2025-10-01 4:57 p.m.
even at 9dbi, your vertical beamwidth is still in the 13-15 degree range. assuming no down tilt, half that and do the math to figure out when that beam hits the ground. it's not as far as you may think. If that works out for you, then that's a good db level to pick
1
4:58 p.m.
find an antenna with some down tilt if you need to hit the ground sooner and longer-distance isn't a primary concern
4:59 p.m.
lora can also get quite a bit out of the portion of the radiation pattern below the -3db point, and side lobes/reflections
Deleted User 2025-10-01 5:19 p.m.
Thanks for sharing this, I found this image that I believe illustrates what you guys said. Sharing for my fellow visual learners .
Cisien
even at 9dbi, your vertical beamwidth is still in the 13-15 degree range. assuming no down tilt, half that and do the math to figure out when that beam hits the ground. it's not as far as you may think. If that works out for you, then that's a good db level to pick
Brock 2025-10-01 5:37 p.m.
I think that might be a bit optimistic. Have a look at the graphic on the Sinclair site: https://www.sinctech.com/products/collinear-omni-9-5-10-dbd-low-pim-hd-900-950-mhz The high gain lobe is really narrow. Yes, there are more lobes below with less gain, but more importantly, there are plenty of nulls where the signal is effectively missing.
Collinear omni, 9.5-10 dBd, low PIM, HD, 900-950 MHz
• 9.5-10 dBd gain with omni-directional pattern and low PIM • Compact lightweight heavy-duty fiberglass radome • 0-6 electrical downtilt available
5:38 p.m.
Cisien 2025-10-01 5:43 p.m.
yeah, not much you can do with the nulls for sure, my main call out was the -3 db beam width
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-10-01 5:56 p.m.
Also the nulls aren't huge areas of no signal, 8db on the roof for example you still get signal at that range
Brock
I think that might be a bit optimistic. Have a look at the graphic on the Sinclair site: https://www.sinctech.com/products/collinear-omni-9-5-10-dbd-low-pim-hd-900-950-mhz The high gain lobe is really narrow. Yes, there are more lobes below with less gain, but more importantly, there are plenty of nulls where the signal is effectively missing.
ka.RFz 2025-10-01 5:56 p.m.
Re the Sinclairs. I discovered last week that you can order those Omni's with different null fill / electrical tilt angles depending on your desired area of coverage / site elevation which would be far better than radiating the majority of the power straight out horizontally.
Brock 2025-10-01 5:56 p.m.
AFAIK, the 0, -10, etc., represent the gain relative to the adverised gain. So assuming it's a 10 dBd antenna, the -10 circle represents 0 dBd gain. Ouch! (edited)
5:58 p.m.
(edited my sticky finger error)
Brock 2025-10-01 6:08 p.m.
And anything inside the -10 circle is less than 0 dBd gain! How much did it cost?? Now here's a Sinclair 0 dBd ground plane antenna. A huge lobe without any nulls. Most of the lobe is just slightly below 0 dB. (edited)
6:12 p.m.
I built my own ground plane with a chassis mount SO239 connector and some chunks of copper wire. It performs extremely well at a cost of close to $0. Bottom line...do not get hung up on antenna gain claims without recognizing the compromise (in terms of both performance and cost) that comes with it. (My rant for the day... )
Brock 2025-10-01 7:10 p.m.
(note that Sinclair uses dBd, which I believe is dB relative to a dipole, which is around 2 dB. Add 2 to dBd numbers to get dBi...which is gain over an isotropic antenna)
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-10-01 7:11 p.m.
2.5, 2.25.. Some thing around there (edited)
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-10-01 7:18 p.m.
2.15

You made me Google... Yes 2.15 looks right!

Cold Weather Charging of Lithium-Ion Batteries: Real-World Lessons ...
By Cully @ KBOXLabs & Elliot Renn Intro: When Theory Meets Reality Ask almost any battery expert, and they’ll tell you one thing for sure: don’t charge lithium-ion batteries below freezin…
1
Cisien 2025-10-18 11:08 p.m.
cell damage from charging can be mitigated by de-rating and charging far below the cell's specified values
11:09 p.m.
and if you use the node to charge the panel, you're probably well below the cell's limits

10:57 a.m.

Cisien 2025-10-20 12:42 p.m.
parallel would get you more current for charging, is that what you want? Can your charger use it?
12:42 p.m.
series would give you higher voltage, which extends the charging time at "the shoulders" (dawn/dusk)
12:43 p.m.
and give a chance of getting enough power to charge in cloudy weather
ded (SalishMesh)

mcarper 2025-11-10 2:47 a.m.
@Mistial Dev I'm interested in the Point Roberts issue. Can you make a summery of what you need in here?
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 2:55 a.m.
Background: At its core, we have a geographic area that's vulnerable due to sociopolitical considerations and geographic considerations. One road in and out, through the border agencies. One ISP, one power company. When the lines on the way in go down, we can lose comms and power. The tower is aging, we control one half and pay for repairs, though the county may take over that at some point. We have the PREP group, which is the prepper group. Many are HAMs. They are focused on taking care of themselves and their neighbors. Free spirits, not under any command or control. We have something like 50 members, 80% of which are Canadians (under training visas, I believe, excluding dual citizens/dual residents). We have very few fires (fortunately!), so we are doing lots of training. We have CERT (Community Emergency Response Team), which is under the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) chain of command, and affiliated with the county. We can activate them. They have training to shut off gas lines, tag people for mass casualties, etc. They will be activated when we need bodies, as was the case with Search and Rescue. Right now, communications is through WhatsAPP. The local agency members have pagers that do two tone. That's the UHF repeater link. We're the front line. CERT is the additional manpower. The department has 3 legs - Emergency Medicine, Search and Rescue, and Fire. PREP does ... things outside those scopes. A bit more independent. We had a SAR activation of CERT, and ran into difficulties getting the word out quickly and effectively at night to people without dedicated pagers, due to silenced phones, people sleeping, muted notifications in the new IOS AI thing, etc. This limited the amount of manpower that could be brought to bear until people woke up.
2:58 a.m.
This brings me to where I'm trying to get: resilient local communications for point roberts, something mesh based. Hardware that is able to activate responders selectively. Multiple channels - FD, CERT, PREP, Water/Utilities. Needs to be fairly reliable, though it is a backup. Text is fine. Needs to have some sort of buzzer/tone/GPIO so I can trigger people. The cheaper the devices are, the more I can get out there, hence my interest in the T-1000E. I want something that people can leave at home, plugged in, where they will get the call when that call comes in and check their phone. If we've had a tree knock out a power line in a wind storm, I'd love to be able to still get the mesage through and have people be able to either see on a screen, or pull up bluetooth.
3:01 a.m.
I have access to a crank-up pole at our fire department. https://www.google.com/maps/place/2030+Benson+Rd,+Point+Roberts,+WA+98281/@48.9888567,-123.0466803,700m/data=!3m2!1e3!4b1!4m6!3m5!1s0x5485e8a9b675e925:0x9344982da79497fa!8m2!3d48.9888567!4d-123.0441!16s%2Fg%2F11w7qq2v2k?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI1MTEwNC4xIKXMDSoASAFQAw%3D%3D An RV park on gulf road https://www.google.com/maps/place/1408+Gulf+Rd,+Point+Roberts,+WA+98281/@48.9868469,-123.0824243,1507m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m10!1m2!2m1!1s1408+gulf+road!3m6!1s0x5485e5f0d1c65bf9:0x51dfc4e39588e516!8m2!3d48.9857303!4d-123.077967!15sCg4xNDA4IGd1bGYgcm9hZJIBEWNvbXBvdW5kX2J1aWxkaW5n4AEA!16s%2Fg%2F11g8_26pwb?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI1MTEwNC4xIKXMDSoASAFQAw%3D%3D A water tower here: https://maps.app.goo.gl/euUVwDEZzcNitzoo8 A water tower here: https://maps.app.goo.gl/euUVwDEZzcNitzoo8 and high likelihood we can access the marina here https://maps.app.goo.gl/euUVwDEZzcNitzoo8 and the tower here for some sort of price https://maps.app.goo.gl/euUVwDEZzcNitzoo8
2030 Benson Rd · 2030 Benson Rd, Point Roberts, WA 98281
Building
Google Maps
Find local businesses, view maps and get driving directions in Google Maps.
49°00'03.8"N 123°04'45.8"W
3:02 a.m.
Ultimately, the #1 thing for us, more than anything else, is to be able to hit a button, and alert people going "we need bodies, right now, to deal with [emergency]". It might be wildfire, it might be a road out, incoming tsunami, whatever.
3:02 a.m.
Something that doesn't need cell, because our cell is spotty.
3:02 a.m.
and half the time you're talking to Canada anyway.
3:06 a.m.
The thing that got me towards meshcore and away from meshtastic is some of the more liberty-minded individuals, shall we say, might wish to play with their radios in ways that are not detrimental to a mesh, and I want to be able to control what's a repeater, what's not. If I've got specific repeaters in specific locations with good coverage, that's great. The earlier discussions about just going two tone are interesting, and I'm considering it. I have a bunch of old XTS radios, and it may make sense to just set up a repeater at the firehall, go analog, and use it as the primary way to do CERT. Or a GMRS repeater. Will still want that backup with mesh, and cheap pagers are appealing. At $50 a T1000E, it's easy enough to hand out a couple dozen, and upgrade people to nicer ones who want to cover the cost themselves. T-Decks, etc.
mcarper 2025-11-10 3:14 a.m.
https://www.seeedstudio.com/XIAO-nRF52840-Wio-SX1262-Kit-for-Meshtastic-p-6400.html this is the cheapest complete radio I'd recommend getting. @loather should know if a buzzer can be added to this. Then you can create a firmware that has your channel baked into it for alerts and can have it trigger a buzzer till X happens to turn off the buzzer. Will need a smaller battery and usb charger so it's plugged in 24/7 but should work a few days with a good sized battery.
XIAO nRF52840 & Wio-SX1262 Kit for Meshtastic
This kit combines the XIAO nRF52840 and Wio-SX1262, supporting BLE5.0 and LoRa (862-930MHz), and operates with low power consumption. It can be applied in various fields such as Meshtastic, sensor networks, environmental monitoring, asset tracking, education, and innovation.
3:18 a.m.
With a different channel you can use the same settings as the rest of the CascadiaMesh network and never have these alert due to them only having that one channel. Too many false alerts will make this not work. This should be cheap enough to make it a device that only does 1 thing
3:20 a.m.
It could be as simple as a power irrupt switch to make the buzzing stop and they read the message on the tag as it has a better waterproof rating and bigger battery vs the t-1000e for the same cost https://store.rokland.com/products/wismesh-tag-from-rakwireless-mokosmart-meshtastic-compatible-card-sized-node-us915-mhz (edited)
WisMesh Tag from RAKwireless & MOKOSmart - Meshtastic compatible card
What is WisMesh Tag? The WisMesh Tag is your compact companion for location tracking and mesh messaging. Built for Meshtastic users who want something portable, reliable, and ready-to-go. Unlike DIY builds or limited designs like the T1000E, the WisMesh Tag delivers reliable outdoor performance with a 1000mAh battery,
mcarper
With a different channel you can use the same settings as the rest of the CascadiaMesh network and never have these alert due to them only having that one channel. Too many false alerts will make this not work. This should be cheap enough to make it a device that only does 1 thing
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:36 a.m.
"Too many false alerts will make this not work. " <- exactly.
3:40 a.m.
If we ran meshtastic, I would add some additional safeguards into it. OTAR is an interesting mechanism.
3:40 a.m.
My concern adding features to meshcore is the FW on the T-Decks.
mcarper
It could be as simple as a power irrupt switch to make the buzzing stop and they read the message on the tag as it has a better waterproof rating and bigger battery vs the t-1000e for the same cost https://store.rokland.com/products/wismesh-tag-from-rakwireless-mokosmart-meshtastic-compatible-card-sized-node-us915-mhz (edited)
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:41 a.m.
That's a nicer unit indeed.
mcarper 2025-11-10 3:41 a.m.
OTAR = Over the Air ?
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:41 a.m.
Rekey, yes.
3:42 a.m.
It's a feature in certain radio systems.
1
3:42 a.m.
The implementation I would do involves a key derivation function, and a couple of "slots" that can be used for wrapping and for channels. AES doesn't need to extend length depending on the implementation.
3:43 a.m.
As an example, "slot 1" might be the active fire department key. Each radio has an OTAR key in "slot 2". Over the course of a month, I might push out messages for each authorized radio to load the upcoming key into slot 3, wrapped with their key.
3:44 a.m.
Then at the swithcover time, you switch the channel to slot 3 from slot 1.
3:45 a.m.
It's not a lot of storage, and a lot of the logic can be done on a computer. Need a way to keep track of what keys are provisioned to what device.
mcarper 2025-11-10 3:47 a.m.
I wouldn't worry about sending out new channels over the air; that makes this 10x harder and just extra hoops for not a lot of gain. In person "updates" is what I'd do since the geography is so small of an area.
mcarper
I wouldn't worry about sending out new channels over the air; that makes this 10x harder and just extra hoops for not a lot of gain. In person "updates" is what I'd do since the geography is so small of an area.
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:47 a.m.
New keys for existing channels.
3:48 a.m.
Used if a device is lost or a person is no longer authorized.
mcarper
I wouldn't worry about sending out new channels over the air; that makes this 10x harder and just extra hoops for not a lot of gain. In person "updates" is what I'd do since the geography is so small of an area.
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:48 a.m.
It's not strictly necessary. I'm just a security engineer and it would be fun
mcarper 2025-11-10 3:49 a.m.
Plug in the device into your computer and update the firmware; new keys
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:49 a.m.
I don't like keys sitting around in devices for a year plus and not knowing who's shared their barcodes and what keys have gotten to what situation.
mcarper
Plug in the device into your computer and update the firmware; new keys
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:50 a.m.
Sure, but if we have 30-40 of these out, some at CERT, some at PREP, some people having loaded their own by exporting the barcode and importing new ones, any key rotation would fragment things and cause issues.
3:51 a.m.
And it's not a particularly complicated feature, and it's one I'd be willing to contribute back.
mcarper 2025-11-10 3:51 a.m.
In the device have it attached to a pi zero w 2 with tailscale so you can remote manage over ip
3:51 a.m.
yeah if you're looking to do that, that'd be cool
3:51 a.m.
meshcore has an access control list
3:52 a.m.
so you could setup the buzzer to only go off if the ACL admin tells it to vs channels
3:52 a.m.
but that'd be a script that DMs every device in short
3:53 a.m.
Rooms might work actually
mcarper
Rooms might work actually
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:55 a.m.
I like the room concept. Going to test to see how reliable delivery is. Done right, I could see it drastically improving reliability of the network.
3:55 a.m.
That might actually be a good place to add some functionality - admin triggered notifications.
mcarper 2025-11-10 3:55 a.m.
It's a system that DMs everyone in short
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:56 a.m.
If the room can designate "this is from an admin"...
mcarper 2025-11-10 3:56 a.m.
rooms can be read only
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:56 a.m.
and filters out messages that claim to be admin but aren't.
mcarper 2025-11-10 3:56 a.m.
rooms have ACL's so only auth people can post
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:57 a.m.
Ok. That actually works great for the CERT side.
3:57 a.m.
With meshtastic, we had notifications on bells. The main thing is people being able to reply, but that's a nice to have not a need to have.
mcarper 2025-11-10 3:57 a.m.
One way communication only using a read only room
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:57 a.m.
I can set up two rooms if needed.
3:58 a.m.
CERT Discussion (no notification), and CERT Activation (ACL)
mcarper 2025-11-10 3:58 a.m.
well you have the room for the buzzer thing and a channel for messages
3:58 a.m.
so it can be 2 way coms
Mistial Dev 2025-11-10 3:59 a.m.
yeah, that's what I was saying
1
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-11-10 8:36 a.m.
Rooms are not reliable enough IMO for something important like this
8:37 a.m.
I have an idea that I should get around to implementing. I want to build a LoRa alert system. One repeater that can optionally handle sending customizable messages over one or more channels for a configurable interval.
8:38 a.m.
Also 0 or multi hop should be an option
8:38 a.m.
For your specific use case, have you seen meshcore SAR?
8:38 a.m.
https://dz0ny.dev/posts/meshcore-sar/
MeshCore SAR | Blog - Janez Troha
Resilient, offline communication for field operations — built on MeshCore and LoRa mesh networking.
8:41 a.m.
The custom firmware for this one only supports a couple devices right now so not quite ready, but optional companion repeating will help a lot in an area with poor coverage during any emergency operations
8:45 a.m.
Regarding the alert system, you could use it for anything. Local level amber alert for kids or even at risk elders for example. Local flooding.

8:45 a.m.
An acl list would be able to login and view, set and delete alert tasks

Then you can create a firmware that has your channel baked into it for alerts and can have it trigger a buzzer till X happens to turn off the buzzer. Will need a smaller battery and usb charger so it's plugged in 24/7 but should work a few days with a good sized battery.
loather 2025-11-10 9:18 a.m.
There are a couple free GPIOs you could do this with. Wouldn't take much firmware hacking to get it set up

Rooms are not reliable enough IMO for something important like this
mcarper 2025-11-10 10:24 a.m.
it would be through 2 hops and that's fairly reliable if the SNR is +5
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-11-10 10:25 a.m.
I have a room one hop away and can rarely get in
10:25 a.m.
It's also very heavy in comparison to a broadcast over a channel
10:25 a.m.

mcarper 2025-11-10 10:26 a.m.
Read only channels don't exist unfortunately
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-11-10 10:26 a.m.
No but wouldn't be needed for the alert system I'm planning
10:28 a.m.
Admin could essentially say 'alert "my message" #channel-name number-of-sends optional-interval" '
10:28 a.m.
Nothing actually decided but something like that
10:30 a.m.

ded (SalishMesh)

BrianJS 2025-12-27 11:25 p.m.
Near Christmas Church Cathedral from inside my place at ground level, on Dec 24 and 25, I was suddenly able to receive hundreds of packets with first hop being 86 and 42. As I did receive 0 hop adverts from Pugetmesh Haystack, I assume it was the 86. I was surprised my little Heltec V3 with a cheap antenna upgrade picked up a signal from 139 km at almost ground level. I could not however transmit. It was a fluke I received anything as I found that moving the antenna a couple of inches up/down/left/right received nothing. I just happened to luck out when I put it where I did that night, hit the sweet spot. I haven't received anything at home since that time from either router. I am not sure what 42 is, think it might be what has been named Repeater 4201 and Infinite Improbability.
2
Jade 2025-12-30 7:54 p.m.
We're getting a lot of test message traffic down here. Would be wonderful if BC people were encouraged to use #testing or a bot channel for tests.
mcarper 2025-12-30 9:28 p.m.
Wardrive also works for position checks
Jade
We're getting a lot of test message traffic down here. Would be wonderful if BC people were encouraged to use #testing or a bot channel for tests.
ritual 2026-01-17 7:00 p.m.
What was the point in you going out of your way to say this if not just to single out a specific group and be disrespectful? I see plenty of testing from WA users on public.
ritual
What was the point in you going out of your way to say this if not just to single out a specific group and be disrespectful? I see plenty of testing from WA users on public.
Jade 2026-01-17 7:11 p.m.
what you see from WA is a direct result of people thinking it was okay to put testing messages in Public because of the BC traffic.
7:11 p.m.
It's not disrespectful to ask to use a testing channel
7:11 p.m.
We do it all the time down here
7:11 p.m.
Infact we even have a bot which asks people to do it
7:12 p.m.
Sorry if you were offended by the request but it that was not my intent.
7:13 p.m.
We are now one mesh, so being able to coordinate on stuff like this is essential.
ritual 2026-01-17 7:14 p.m.
To pass the blame onto BC uers like that is gross. You made a broad generalization based on one individual, and that is entirely disresptful.
ritual
To pass the blame onto BC uers like that is gross. You made a broad generalization based on one individual, and that is entirely disresptful.
Jade 2026-01-17 7:15 p.m.

7:15 p.m.
It's just growing pains.
7:15 p.m.

i send the occasional test message over public because i'm lazy, not because i see bc people doing it

Jade 2026-01-17 7:16 p.m.

7:17 p.m.
When it started people in BC didn't know we could hear them.
7:17 p.m.
So that was the intent
7:18 p.m.
Have a great day, I do not intend to discuss this beyond this because the problem was solved.

Cisien 2026-01-17 11:11 p.m.
I noticed that Haystack and cougar are starting to creek under the load of the mesh. I think we should all review our advert settings and make sure 0-hop is disabled, and flood is at least 24 hours. 48 ideal.
ded (SalishMesh) 2026-01-17 11:14 p.m.
Do you remember what utilization was when it was ever few hours flooding?
11:14 p.m.
And what it is now?
11:14 p.m.
Someone was just talking about this is a local signal chat and I couldn't remember the numbers
Cisien 2026-01-17 11:15 p.m.
i don't have the data anymore
11:16 p.m.
adverts are the bulk of what haystack and cougar (and any high elevation node) will hear
11:16 p.m.
and they also hear most of the 0-hop adverts
ded (SalishMesh) 2026-01-17 11:16 p.m.
Wild
11:16 p.m.
But it was more than just a percent or two
Cisien 2026-01-17 11:19 p.m.
airtime usage at cougar has steadily grown ever since we got going. adverts have always been the bulk. the HA extension going wild was the big cause of our last bit of congestion
ded (SalishMesh) 2026-01-17 11:21 p.m.
I thought so, I remember seeing graphs with advert traffic
Cisien 2026-01-17 11:23 p.m.
this is what i see at my house

11:24 p.m.

11:24 p.m.

11:24 p.m.

1
MrAlders0n 2026-01-18 1:38 p.m.
Popping in from MeshMapper. I understand TX wardriving isn't ideal for mesh load. We've built in safeguards to address this. Each region gets an admin panel with a designated POC who can limit TX wardriving slots. Even disable and stop all active wardriving if they deem required. We felt like we had to build in this admin function as we were worried about Mesh load. When users attempt to connect and no slots are available, they automatically fall back to passive wardriving only. Slots free up as other users disconnect. This approach prevents our app from overwhelming the mesh with transmit activity while still enabling active data collection when capacity allows. (edited)
2
1:39 p.m.
In Ottawa for example we limit it to 5 currently but other regions may need way less like Salish mesh for example

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-18 8:16 p.m.
I’ll try the scheduled invitations for a while. I wanted to make sure the message wasn’t an ad for my bot but info on how to find the bots for testing. ritual 2026-01-18 8:24 p.m.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-18 8:28 p.m.

Ah very good. I caretake the VE7UBC (1c) repeater, happy to run up some tests with you when you are ready. There was talk a couple months back about throwing a repeater up on the top of Leading Peak repeater site on Anvil Island. Would be good to have a couple more repeaters up Howe Sound for sure.
d.blair
Thanks for the suggested channels. I have a site on Gambier that's LoS to UBC (1c) and BrohmRidge repeaters. North Shore (Canyon Heights) with LoS from UBC to Metrotown.
ka.RFz 2026-01-26 6:54 p.m.
Also just dropped you a DM with some other info. Cheers

Deleted User 2026-01-29 2:47 a.m.
Has anyone in here made a pitch to their municipal govt about purchasing/installing large repeaters on top of infrastructure in parks or public buildings, and then using partnerships with local radio or disaster prep orgs, or just normal people to encourage residents to buy personal nodes? Obviously the tech isn't ready for normie prime time, but considering the state of the US right now perhaps it might be time for our local communities to start building result comms networks ASAP, doing our best to create real (and cheap) increase that normal people can connect to in worst case scenarios? (edited)
2:48 a.m.
Also if anyone in here is in Langley or the Fraser Valley, please reach out! I'd love to chat and connect!
d.blair 2026-01-29 2:55 a.m.
Govt are inherently risk adverse. I approached one 2 years ago. Their issues we're; hosting could be seen as endorsing a network that they have no control over how it's used, they don't have direct benifits to participating in, and could interfere with existing emergency comms. It was a hard sell and ultimately a "no". But I'd encourage trying!
2

Just Stuff™ 2026-02-09 8:08 a.m.
Good morning fellow meshengers I've been working on a free new tool to visualize actual repeater coverage using the node discover feature. It draws color coded hexagonal layers on a map feel free to use it at will. All that is required for initial setup is a meshcore ble companion radio. It runs in your browser online or offline with optional map caching. What Is Mesh Utility? Mesh Utility helps you see how well your LoRa mesh radio network covers an area. Walk or drive around with your radio connected, and the app builds a color-coded map showing where your signal is strong, weak, or missing entirely. On iPhones and iPads, you'll need the Bluefy or WebBLE browser since Safari doesn't support this type of Bluetooth. On Android, computers, or Chromebooks, use Chrome or Edge. Once connected, the app shows your radio's name, battery level, and frequency at the top of the settings panel. The screen will stay awake while connected so you don't lose your Bluetooth link during scans. If you put the app in the background (especially on iOS), the Bluetooth connection may drop. When you return, the app checks automatically and lets you know if you need to reconnect. The app sends out a discovery signal and listens for any repeaters that respond, measuring how strong and clear each signal is. Each scan cycle takes about 20 seconds. Your GPS location (and altitude when available) is recorded with each scan so the map knows exactly where you were. Green means excellent signal. Yellow-green is good. Yellow is fair. Orange is marginal. Red is poor. Dark red means no repeater responded at all (a dead zone). Purple means the signal power is okay but clarity is bad, usually from interference. Tap any hex to see its details: signal readings, how many scans were taken there, altitude, and which repeaters were heard. https://mesh-utility.replit.app/manual
Mesh Utility - LoRa MeshCore Radio Manager
Connect to mesh radios, discover nodes, and visualize RF coverage.

Dan - WI7FI [PNW] 2026-02-11 10:20 p.m.
nope no advertisements needed to participate in channel chats. you can check here to see if your message is making it to the public channel: https://analyzer.letsmesh.net/channels
MeshCore Analyzer - LetsMesh.net
Real-time MeshCore mesh network analytics, node tracking, packet analysis, and coverage maps.
10:20 p.m.
make sure your region is set to "Pacific Northwest" on the analyzer page (edited)

SM- 2026-03-15 11:55 p.m.
Anyone can verify issues with coverage by F44D (UBC Zenith) repeater or where it subsequently connects to? For the past two days, I have been having issues with getting out
Deleted User
Hi friends! I'm very new to the Meshcore world and live in Langley City, BC, which I can see on the Salishmesh coverage map is a relative desert. I don't have any hardware whatsoever yet, as this is a brand new rabbit hole for me, but I am very excited about getting involved here! I'd be very interested in doing some community work to see if there's anyone willing to put up a repeater in the area!
SM- 2026-03-16 12:14 a.m.
Hi! Did you end up putting up a repeater in Langely area?
SM-
Anyone can verify issues with coverage by F44D (UBC Zenith) repeater or where it subsequently connects to? For the past two days, I have been having issues with getting out
ka.RFz 2026-03-16 4:41 a.m.
Hey there, I maintain the Zenith repeater. From what I can see through my remote management and the letsmesh analyzer, Zenith is online and passing packets around as usual.
1
1
SM- 2026-03-16 9:52 a.m.
Thank you, maybe it’s something wrong on my end. I was able to repeat via it, now I see it but my companion device says “no repeaters found” when tx messages. Going to try something else tonight.
SM-
Thank you, maybe it’s something wrong on my end. I was able to repeat via it, now I see it but my companion device says “no repeaters found” when tx messages. Going to try something else tonight.
ka.RFz 2026-03-16 3:35 p.m.
It's possible that there was a huge dump of snow up where it is and the signal may be slightly attenuated. From what I can see, the SNR from it is slightly lower than it was when I installed it in January (there was no snow up there at the time).

CascadiaMesh is a community dedicated to the growth, promotion and maintenance of a decentralized MeshCore network covering British Columbia, Washington State and Oregon and beyond.

bourbonator 2026-03-31 10:28 a.m.
Good morning PNW! I just wanted to post to let you all know that Mesh America received it's 501c3 designation letter from the IRS this week, and that we're full speed ahead on the I5 project. If you want to learn more about the I-5 Project: https://meshamerica.com/the-i-5-project/ I am working on social media pages, but so far only the Facebook page is up if you want to follow us: https://www.facebook.com/meshamerica TLDR: We're focused on building out mesh networks to connect major metros, and to provide communications networks to rural communities. The I5 is our pilot project to set a process, and prove it can be done.
The I-5 Project
Building the Backbone of CascadiaMesh
Log in or sign up to view
See posts, photos and more on Facebook.

I’m doing some network planning and I am wondering if anyone knows how high up the Joyce Repeater in Vancouver is?
Meshtache
I’m doing some network planning and I am wondering if anyone knows how high up the Joyce Repeater in Vancouver is?
ka.RFz 2026-04-02 11:34 a.m.
The owner is on a other group chat and had posted photos a few months back. It's on a highrise balcony that is prominent over the area with LOS to the north west.
Uncle Lit 2026-04-23 2:27 p.m.
the meshcore dev team have moved to a new discord server. details and reasons are here: https://blog.meshcore.io/2026/04/23/the-split Please create a new thread for your region in the https://discord.com/channels/1495203904898728149/1495412453150818585 for your region on the new meshcore.io discord server. https://meshcore.gg
